title: Open-Frame Laundry Basket Organizer
summary: A 2x4 frame that turns a stack of laundry baskets into sliding drawers — sized off YOUR baskets, with a slatted top that doubles as a folding counter.
description:
The laundry basket is already a drawer; it just has nothing to slide in. This organizer is an open 2x4 frame with runners spaced to your baskets, so each one slides out, rides to the bedroom, and slides back — no drawer boxes, no slides, no hardware. The slatted 1x6 top adds a folding surface, and the gaps between top boards are deliberate: airflow, and somewhere to drape things to dry.

This plan has no fixed cut list, and that's not a gap — it's the design. Every dimension derives from three measurements of the basket you actually own: overall height, depth on the sliding side, and the width under the lip (the lip is what rides the runners). The steps carry the formulas; the source build used the common rectangular bushel-style basket and came out around 29" tall for two baskets per column. Buy identical baskets first, then build the furniture around them — it's the only order that works.

Construction is 2-1/2" screws and glue, all straight cuts. If your laundry room floor is anything but flat (most aren't), check the frame for rock before the top goes on and shim a leg then, not after.

tools.note: The whole plan is three careful measurements of your basket — this is the most important tool in the build.

materials.name: 2x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Legs, horizontal frames, and runners — as shown, for a 2x2 basket arrangement. Taller/wider versions need more.

materials.name: Laundry baskets, rectangular with lip
materials.note: Buy these FIRST — identical, bushel-style. Every dimension in the build comes from them. Not counted in the cost estimate.

steps.title: Measure your baskets, derive your cuts
steps.body:
Three measurements off one basket drive everything:

LEG LENGTH: basket height plus 2" is the clearance per basket (typically ~13"). Multiply by baskets per column (two shown, so ~26"), then add 3" for the top and bottom supports — about 29" for two-high, 42" for three-high. Add more if you're targeting a specific counter height.

RUNNERS: the basket's DEPTH on the sliding side — two runners per basket.

HORIZONTAL FRAMES: the width under the basket's lip plus 3" — four of them.

Cut four legs and four frame pieces from 2x4, and screw each frame between a pair of legs (frames inside the legs) with 2-1/2" screws. Build two identical leg frames.

steps.title: Mark and mount the runners
steps.body:
The runners are what the basket lips ride on, so their spacing is the whole trick:

BOTTOM runner: its TOP edge sits one full basket height (lip included) up from the floor line.

EVERY runner above it: one basket height PLUS 2" above the one below.

Mark both leg frames together, side by side, so left and right runners land dead level with each other — a basket on staggered runners binds. Attach with 2-1/2" screws and glue.

Slide a basket in before moving on. If it drags, now is when you fix it.

steps.title: Add the slatted top
steps.body:
Cut 1x6 top boards to match the basket depth, space them evenly across the two frames, and attach with the 1-3/4" screws.

Keep real gaps between boards — the airflow keeps damp laundry from souring, and the slats double as a drying rack for anything laid flat. Sand the top well; it's about to become the folding counter.

images.alt: Open wood frame organizer holding sliding laundry baskets like drawers, with slatted top
